chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 785 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْعِيصُ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ ، أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فِي ثَوْبِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ أَوْ إِزَارِهَا وَ يَعْتَمُّ بِخِمَارِهَا فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ إِذَا كَانَتْ مَأْمُونَةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.785 - Al-Ays ibn Al-Qasim asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man praying in a woman's garment or her waist wrap, or wrapping himself with her headcover.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if it is clean (from any impurity)”.
